description: Two-tier grounding cascade. Tier 1: regex denylist + content-word overlap (stdlib, <5 ms). Tier 2: LLM-as-judge using the pipeline's main text model. Replaces the previous 3-tier MiniCheck NLI + Lynx-8B cascade (retired 2026-05-01). Bind-mounted into the container at /root/.openclaw/grounding.json. Runtime override via CLIP_GROUNDING_CONFIG env var.

tier_1.description: Regex denylist + content-word overlap. Stdlib only, always available. Hard-event check (Phase 2.4d) short-circuits when a denylist hit's keyword maps to a zero-count event in chat — that's the single sharpest anti-hallucination signal and runs regardless of judge availability.

judge.description: LLM-as-judge using the pipeline's main text model. Resolves via cfg.model -> CLIP_GROUNDING_JUDGE_MODEL -> CLIP_TEXT_MODEL -> qwen/qwen3.5-9b fallback. Returns 5-dimensional scores (grounding/setup_payoff/speaker/conceptual/callback) collapsed to a 0-10 weighted mean. Pass = weighted >= pass_threshold.
judge.model_description: null = resolve at runtime to whatever CLIP_TEXT_MODEL the pipeline loaded. Override here only if you want grounding to use a different model than detection.
judge._self_judging_note: The same model that generates Pass B 'why' / Stage 6 title is now also the judge. Self-judging tends to inflate faithfulness ratings 5-15 pp vs an independent model. Tier 1's hard-event check (event_contradicts_ground_truth) is the structural safety net that no LLM rubber-stamping can defeat.

regeneration.description: On a Stage 6 grounding fail, retry the vision call ONCE with a stricter prompt that names the violation. If the retry also fails, null the field and fall back to transcript defaults.
